title: The Battle for Sunken Treasure off Cape Canaveral
subtitle: 卡纳维拉尔角沉船宝藏之争：发现者该得多少

paragraphs.en: Most visitors come to Cape Canaveral, on the northeast coast of Florida, for the tourist attractions. It's home to the second-busiest cruise ship port in the world and is a gateway to the cosmos. Nearly 1.5 million visitors flock here every year to watch rockets, spacecraft, and satellites blast off into the solar system from Kennedy Space Center Visitor Complex. Nearly 64 kilometers of undeveloped beach and 648 square kilometers of protected refuge fan out from the cape's sandy shores.
paragraphs.zh: 大多数游客来到佛罗里达州东北海岸的卡纳维拉尔角，是为了这里的旅游景点。它是全球第二繁忙的邮轮港口所在地，也是一扇通往宇宙的门户。每年有近150万游客涌入这里，在肯尼迪航天中心游客园区观看火箭、航天器和卫星发射升空进入太阳系。近64公里未开发的海滩和648平方公里的受保护野生动物保护区从海角的沙滩向外延展开来。

paragraphs.en: Yet some of Cape Canaveral's most legendary attractions lie unseen, wedged under the sea's surface in mud and sand, for this part of the world has a reputation as a deadly ship trap. Over the centuries, dozens of majestic Old World sailing ships smashed and sank on this irregular stretch of windy Florida coast. They were vessels built for war and commerce, crossing the globe carrying everything from coins to cannons, boxes of silver and gold, chests of jewels and porcelain, and pearls from the Caribbean.
paragraphs.zh: 然而，卡纳维拉尔角一些最具传奇色彩的景点却深藏不露，埋在海底的泥沙之下，因为这片海域素有致命船只陷阱的名声。几个世纪以来，数十艘雄伟的旧大陆帆船在这段不规则且多风的佛罗里达海岸上撞毁沉没。这些船只为战争和贸易而造，曾载着从硬币到大炮、成箱的金银、满箱的珠宝瓷器以及加勒比海的珍珠，穿梭于全球各地。

paragraphs.en: Cape Canaveral contains one of the greatest concentrations of colonial shipwrecks in the world. In recent years, advances in radar, diving, detection equipment, computers, and GPS have transformed the hunt. The naked eye might see a pile of rocks, but technology can reveal the precious artifacts that lie hidden on the ocean floor. As technology renders the seabed more accessible, the hunt for treasure-filled ships has drawn a fresh tide of salvors and their investors — as well as marine archaeologists wanting to bring to light the lost relics.
paragraphs.zh: 卡纳维拉尔角是世界上殖民时期沉船最集中的区域之一。近年来，雷达、潜水、探测设备、计算机和GPS技术的进步彻底改变了搜寻方式。肉眼或许只能看到一堆石头，但技术却能揭示隐藏在海底的珍贵文物。随着技术让海底变得更容易触及，对满载宝藏船只的搜寻吸引了新一波打捞者及其投资者，同时也引来了希望让失落文物重见天日的海洋考古学家。

paragraphs.en: But of late, when salvors have found vessels, their rights have been challenged in court. The big question: who should have control of these treasures? High-stakes fights over shipwrecks pit archaeologists against treasure hunters in a vicious cycle of accusations. Archaeologists regard themselves as protectors of history, and they see salvors as careless destroyers. Salvors feel they do the hard work of searching for ships, only to have them stolen from under them when discovered.
paragraphs.zh: 但最近，当打捞者发现沉船后，他们的权利却在法庭上受到了挑战。最大的问题是：谁应该拥有对这些宝藏的控制权？围绕沉船的高风险争斗使考古学家与寻宝者陷入相互指责的恶性循环。考古学家自视为历史的保护者，认为打捞者是粗心大意的破坏者。打捞者则觉得他们辛辛苦苦搜寻沉船，结果一旦发现就被别人从眼皮底下抢走。

words.meaning: adj. 高风险的；赌注大的

paragraphs.en: This kind of clash inevitably takes place on a grand scale. Aside from the salvors, their investors, and the maritime archaeologists who serve as expert witnesses, the battles sweep in local and international governments and organizations like UNESCO that work to protect underwater heritage. The court cases that ensue stretch on for years. Are finders keepers, or do the ships belong to the countries that made them and sent them sailing centuries ago? Where once salvors and archaeologists worked side by side, now they belong to opposing, and equally contemptuous, tribes.
paragraphs.zh: 此类冲突不可避免地会大规模上演。除了打捞者、其投资者以及作为专家证人的海洋考古学家之外，战火还卷入了地方及国际政府，以及致力于保护水下遗产的联合国教科文组织等机构。随之而来的诉讼往往旷日持久。究竟是“谁捡到归谁”，还是这些船只应属于几个世纪前建造并派遣它们出航的国家？打捞者和考古学家曾经并肩工作，如今却分属两个相互对立、彼此鄙夷的阵营。

paragraphs.en: Nearly three million vessels lie wrecked on the Earth's ocean floor — from old canoes to the Titanic — and likely less than one percent have been explored. Some — like an ancient Roman ship found off Antikythera, Greece, dated between 70 and 60 BC and carrying astonishingly sophisticated gears and dials for navigating by the sun — are critical to a new understanding of our past. No wonder there is an eternal stirring among everybody from salvors to scholars to find them.
paragraphs.zh: 地球海底沉睡着近三百万艘失事船只——从古老的独木舟到泰坦尼克号——而其中可能只有不到百分之一被探索过。有些沉船，比如在希腊安提基西拉岛附近发现的一艘古罗马沉船，其年代可追溯至公元前70至60年，船上载有用于靠太阳导航的惊人精密齿轮和刻度盘，对于重新认识我们的过去至关重要。难怪从打捞者到学者，人人都对找到它们抱有永恒的渴望。

paragraphs.en: In May 2016, a salvor named Bobby Pritchett, president of Global Marine Exploration in Tampa, Florida, announced that he had discovered scattered remains of a ship buried a kilometer off Cape Canaveral. Over the prior three years, he and his crew had obtained 14 state permits to survey a nearly 260-square-kilometer area off the cape; they worked 250 days a year, backed by investor funds of, he claims, US $4 million. It was hard work. Crew members were up at dawn, dragging sensors from their expedition vessels back and forth, day in and day out, year after year, to detect metal of any kind.
paragraphs.zh: 2016年5月，佛罗里达州坦帕市全球海洋勘探公司总裁、打捞者博比·普里切特宣布，他在卡纳维拉尔角外海一公里处发现了一艘散落船只的残骸。此前三年间，他和团队获得了14份州政府许可，对海角附近近260平方公里的海域进行了勘测；他们每年工作250天，据他声称依靠400万美元的投资资金支持。工作十分艰苦。船员们黎明即起，驾驶勘测船拖着传感器来回穿梭，日复一日，年复一年，以探测任何种类的金属。

paragraphs.en: Using computer technology, Pritchett and his crew created intricate, color-coded maps marked with the GPS coordinates of thousands of finds, all invisible under a meter of sand. One day in 2015, the magnetometer picked up metal that turned out to be an iron cannon; when the divers blew the sand away, they also discovered a more precious bronze cannon with markings indicating French royalty and, not far off, a famous marble column carved with the coat of arms of France, known from historical paintings.
paragraphs.zh: 借助计算机技术，普里切特和他的团队绘制了精细的彩色编码地图，上面标有数千处发现的GPS坐标，而这些物件全都深埋在一米厚的沙子之下，肉眼无法看见。2015年的一天，磁力仪探测到金属物，结果是一门铁炮；潜水员吹开沙子后，还发现了一门更珍贵的青铜炮，上面带有表明法国王室的标记，不远处还有一根著名的大理石柱，上面雕刻着法国国徽，其图案在历史画作中为人所熟知。

paragraphs.en: The discovery was cause for celebration. The artifacts indicated the divers had likely found the wreck of La Trinity, a 16th-century French vessel that had been at the center of a bloody battle between France and Spain that changed the fate of the United States of America. And then the legal storm began, with GME and Pritchett pitted against Florida and France. The Sunken Military Craft Act of 2004, a US federal act, protects any vessel that was on a military mission, allowing the originating country to claim their ship even centuries later. In 2018, two long years after Pritchett's discovery, the federal district court ruled in favor of France. For Pritchett, the decision was devastating. Millions of dollars of investor funding and years of labor were lost.
paragraphs.zh: 这一发现本值得庆贺。这些文物表明，潜水员很可能找到了“三一”号的残骸，这是一艘16世纪的法国船只，曾处于法国与西班牙之间一场血腥战争的中心，而那场战争改变了美利坚合众国的命运。随后法律风暴骤起，GME公司和普里切特与佛罗里达州及法国对簿公堂。美国2004年《沉没军用船舰法案》这项联邦法律保护任何执行军事任务的船只，允许原属国甚至在数百年后认领其船只。2018年，在普里切特发现沉船整整两年后，联邦地区法院做出了有利于法国的裁决。对普里切特来说，这一判决是毁灭性的。数百万美元的投资者资金和多年的辛劳付诸东流。

paragraphs.en: But this is far from the first time a salvor has lost all rights to a discovery. In 2012, for instance, Spain won a five-year legal battle against Odyssey Marine Exploration, which had hauled 594,000 gold and silver coins from a Spanish wreck off the coast of Portugal across the Atlantic to the United States. "Treasure hunters can be naive," says attorney David Concannon, who has had several maritime archaeologists as clients and represented two sides in the battles over the Titanic for 20 years. "Many treasure hunters don't understand they are going to have to fight for their rights against a government that has an endless supply of money for legal battles that treasure hunters are likely to lose."
paragraphs.zh: 但这远非打捞者首次失去对发现物的全部权利。例如，2012年西班牙就赢得了与奥德赛海洋勘探公司长达五年的诉讼，后者从葡萄牙海岸附近一艘西班牙沉船上打捞出594000枚金银币，并运到了美国。“寻宝者可能很天真，”律师戴维·康坎农说。他的客户中包括多位海洋考古学家，并在长达20年的泰坦尼克号争端中代表过双方。“许多寻宝者不明白，他们将不得不与政府争夺权利，而政府拥有无穷无尽的资金来打官司，寻宝者则很可能会输。”

paragraphs.en: Putting an inflated price on artifacts rather than viewing them as cultural and historical treasures that transcend any price is what irritates many archaeologists. For the archaeologist, everything in a wreck matters — hair, fabric, a fragment of a newspaper, rat bones — all things speak volumes. Archaeologists don't want artifacts ending up in a private collection instead of taking humanity on a journey of understanding.
paragraphs.zh: 给文物标上虚高的价格，而非将其视为超越一切价格的文化历史珍宝，这正是激怒许多考古学家的地方。对考古学家而言，沉船中的一切都有意义——头发、织物、一片报纸碎片、老鼠骨头——一切都能讲述丰富的故事。考古学家不希望文物最终落入私人收藏，而不是引领人类踏上理解历史的旅程。

paragraphs.en: George Bass is one of the pioneers of underwater archaeology, and a researcher at Texas A&M University. He has testified in court against treasure hunters, but says archaeology is not without its own serious problems. He believes archaeologists need to do a better job themselves instead of routinely criticizing treasure hunters. "Archaeology has a terrible reputation for not publishing enough on its excavations and finds," he says. Gathering data, unearthing and meticulously preserving and examining finds, verifying identity and origin, piecing together the larger story, and writing and publishing a comprehensive paper or book can take decades.
paragraphs.zh: 乔治·巴斯是水下考古学的先驱之一，也是得克萨斯农工大学的研究员。他曾在法庭上作证反对寻宝者，但也表示考古学自身也存在严重问题。他认为考古学家需要把本职工作做得更好，而不是例行公事地批评寻宝者。“考古学因其发掘和发现成果发表得不够多而名声不佳，”他说。收集数据、发掘并精心保存和研究发现物、核实其身份与来源、拼凑出更大的历史图景，再到撰写并发表全面的论文或专著，这一切可能需要数十年时间。

paragraphs.en: A bit cynically, Bass describes colleagues who never published because they waited so long they became ill or died. Who is more at fault, Bass asks, the professional archaeologist who carefully excavates a site and never publishes on it or the treasure hunter who locates a submerged wreck, salvages part, conserves part, and publishes a book on the operation? Pritchett concedes that his find deserves careful excavation and preservation. "I think what I found should go in a museum," he says. "But I also think I should get paid for what I found."
paragraphs.zh: 巴斯略带讽刺地描述了那些因等待太久而生病或去世、最终未能发表成果的同事。他问道：谁的责任更大？是那位仔细发掘遗址却从未发表报告的专业考古学家，还是那位定位水下沉船、打捞部分、保存部分并就此出版一本书的寻宝者？普里切特承认，他的发现值得仔细发掘和保存。他说：“我认为我发现的东西应该进博物馆。但我也认为我应该为自己的发现获得报酬。”

paragraphs.en: Indeed, it's a bit of a mystery why governments, archaeologists, and treasure hunters can't work together — and why salvors aren't at least given a substantial finder's fee before the original owner takes possession of the vessel and its artifacts.
paragraphs.zh: 的确，为何政府、考古学家和寻宝者就不能携手合作，以及为何在船只及其文物的原主占有这些物件之前，打捞者至少得不到一笔可观的发现费，这多少有些令人费解。
